description: Accelerated introduction to  foundational materials concepts and the materials paradigm approach to problem solving and research. Atomic scale structure of materials; defects in crystalline materials; alloy phase diagrams, solid-state diffusion, phase transformations, microstructure development, micro/nano/atomic-scale structure-property relationships.

prerequisites.prerequisites_text: Graduate/professional standing. Not open to students with credit inM S & E 350,351, or352.
